parsed_review.summary: Read-only review of CP00-777 (RP25 Migration Platform permission-matrix fixture foundation; risk A; 10 units = 8 implementation + 2 security_audit; range RP25.P06.M05.S29-RP25.P06.M06.S08). Evidence basis: source inspected via Read/Grep/Glob plus the recorded command-evidence.json; commands were NOT re-executed (expected read_tools_used mode). The pack is genuinely descriptor-only and the pack-level descriptor-verified claim is supported. Verified: (1) createCaseSet/createDescriptor (packages/migration/src/service.js) stamp every row with descriptor_only=true, runtime_execution=false, and deny raw/source-system/external-credential/real-client payloads plus all file-server/SharePoint/Drive/iManage/import runtime-opened flags; MIGRATION_PLATFORM_NO_WRITE_ATTESTATION (writes_product_state=false, no DB rows, no object storage, no external runtime) is applied to every result. (2) Authority boundary correct: descriptor authority_boundary sets claude_is_final_approval=false, human_final_approval_required_for_runtime_opening=true, runtime_opening_pack_id=CP00-778, local_validation_claims_enterprise_trust=false; Claude packets read_only=true and promotes_claude_to_final_approval=false. (3) Hermes patterns are deterministic control-plane only: evidence packets emit_runtime_receipt=false, writes_product_state=false, receipt_shape=descriptor_only_migration_no_write; the contract validator writes only the build artifact, not product state. (4) Coverage/distribution exact: deliverable counts 8 impl + 2 security_audit, micro_title 2/8, M05 rows ["Command rerun","Risk register update"] and M06 the 8 fixture rows align with the plan units and the PERMISSION_MATRIX arrays; contracts/migration-platform-contract.json shows cp777 coverage and descriptor both valid:true/errors:[] and latest_projection=MigrationPlatformCp777PermissionMatrixFixtureFoundationDescriptor. (5) Both security_audit deliverables (Permission matrix row, Audit hint fields) inspected in source: descriptor-only with base no-leak posture; descriptor validator rejects exposed permission decisions, audit event bodies, cross-tenant access, unauthorized-count leaks, runtime-render claims, and unverified knowledge creation. (6) Tests/validation evidence: migration model.test.js 67/67 pass, npm test 3015/3015 pass (0 fail), rp25:migration-platform:validate reports contract validation passed (pack CP00-777, next CP00-778), and all ten required commands plus extras recorded exit_code 0 with git diff --check clean. (7) Gates intact: manifest honestly reports production_ready=false and runtime_ready=false pending this review; no runtime or enterprise-trust claim is made, so the human/enterprise-trust final-approval boundary correctly stays outside this receipt. Claude remained a read-only reviewer, not final approver. No P0/P1/P2 issues; one P3 by-design observation noted. No valid-evidence-rejection conditions (auth ok, non-empty stdout, well-formed JSON) apply.

findings.title: Live-pack coverage validates against manifest plan_binding_snapshot (by design)
findings.details: planPack() resolves the CP00-777 binding via plan.packs.find(...) ?? manifest.plan_binding_snapshot. Because docs/closeout-pack-plan/closeout-pack-plan.json lists CP00-777 only under live_state and its packs[] array begins at the next pack (CP00-778), the live pack's coverage check falls back to its own manifest snapshot rather than an independent plan.packs entry. This is mildly self-referential. It is mitigated and acceptable: validateMigrationPlatformCp777PermissionMatrixFixtureFoundationCoverage still checks the snapshot's included_units against registry requirements (deliverable_counts, micro_title_row_counts, required_section_rows derived from PERMISSION_MATRIX arrays), the manifest records source_ledger_sha, and the behavior is identical for every prior pack CP00-756..CP00-776. It is the established live-pack pattern, not a regression introduced by this pack.
findings.recommendation: No action required for closeout. Optionally, for stronger independence, cross-check the live weighted ledger units for the current pack id when the plan does not yet list it in packs[].
